stories.title: Trump threatens NATO allies over Strait of Hormuz help
stories.description: With the Iran war entering a third week and the price of oil reaching nearly $105 a barrel on Monday, President Trump again urged NATO countries and China to help the U.S. secure the vital Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Iran blocks vital oil route as Israel expands its ground operations in Lebanon
stories.description: As the war in Iran enters its third week, there's no end in sight. Iran has blocked the Strait of Hormuz, a vital oil route, as Trump urges allies to act and Israel expands its ground operations.

stories.title: Iran expert on what the country's leaders think about the war with the US and Israel
stories.description: NPR's Michel Martin asks Iran expert Karim Sadjadpour how leaders in Tehran are thinking about the war with the U.S. and Israel.

stories.title: 5th member of Iranian women's soccer team gives up asylum in Australia
stories.description: The player's departure shortly before midnight on Sunday leaves two of an initial seven squad members in Australia.

stories.title: An Iranian-American writer describes the "vertigo" he feels about the war
stories.description: Host Adrian Ma speaks with Iranian American writer Nick Mafi about the war in Iran. Mafi says many Iranians in the US are feeling a sense of vertigo because of the conflict.

stories.title: 'We never asked for a ceasefire,' says Iran's foreign minister, as war keeps raging
stories.description: Israel announced a barrage of new strikes on western Iran on Sunday, while Iran's foreign minister said the country has not asked for a ceasefire as President Trump had claimed.

stories.title: Iran taking steps to prevent anti-establishment protests, Tehran residents tell BBC
stories.description: Residents describe new security checkpoints around the capital, where they say people are stopped and searched.

stories.title: What Iranians are being told about the war
stories.description: Iranian state media's coverage of the war includes inflations of enemy casualties and digital manipulation intended to glorify Iran.

stories.title: Iran captain latest footballer to drop asylum bid, Iranian state media says
stories.description: Five of the seven members of the women's football delegation who originally sought to stay in Australia have now chosen to return home.

stories.title: US names six crew killed in refuelling plane crash in Iraq
stories.description: The aircraft was on a combat mission as part of ongoing US operations against Iran when it crashed in western Iraq on Thursday.

stories.title: BBC visits aftermath of Israeli strike on Lebanon that killed family as IDF targets Hezbollah
stories.description: A relative tells BBC those killed were civilians and not Hezbollah operatives, but the Israel Defense Forces says it was targeting "terrorist infrastructure".

stories.title: Why has the US targeted Iran's Kharg Island?
stories.description: The tiny island is home to one of the most critical pieces of Iran's energy infrastructure.

stories.title: Hamas urges key ally Iran to halt attacks on Gulf states
stories.description: The Palestinian group also affirms Tehran's right to defend itself from "aggression" by the US and Israel.

stories.title: Under drone fire, exiled Kurds wait to confront Iranian regime
stories.description: A group of fighters based in Iraq say they are "ready to go home", as they face drone attacks from Tehran.

stories.title: 'There's no hiding place on a ship': The sailors stranded near Iran
stories.description: Drones, cruise missiles and fighter jets have become a common sight for many sailors stranded on ships in the Gulf.

stories.title: Bodies of 84 Iranian sailors killed in US torpedo strike to be repatriated
stories.description: The seamen died when Iranian warship the Iris Dena was sunk on 4 March by a US submarine near Sri Lanka.

stories.title: Emirati minister tells BBC Iran must end strikes on Gulf
stories.description: The UAE says more than 1,800 drones and missiles have been projected at the country since the war began.

stories.title: 'Fingers on the trigger': Deadly warnings for Iranians being urged to take action
stories.description: Iran's police chief has warned that his forces would treat anyone taking to the streets "at the enemy's request" as an enemy.

stories.title: Why did US and Israel attack Iran and how long could the war last?
stories.description: Conflict has spread across the Middle East since the US and Israel launched wide-ranging strikes on Iran on 28 February.

stories.title: How Iranians are evading internet blocks to contact family abroad
stories.description: Iranians are finding tech-savvy ways to get through the regime's restrictions on phone and internet connections.

stories.title: As hopes of regime change in Iran fade, Netanyahu faces political test
stories.description: Israeli leaders are framing the bombing campaign as having transformed the Middle East in their favour as pressure to end the conflict builds.

stories.title: Answering Trump's call for help in Middle East 'political suicide' for allies
stories.description: President Donald Trump urged NATO partners and China to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz as it remains closed due to the ongoing war in the Middle East triggered by the US-Israeli military campaign against Iran. FRANCE 24's Oliver Farry looks at why Trump's plan could be easier s...

stories.title: IDF launches 'limited operation' in Lebanon as 'buffer zone expands significantly'
stories.description: The Israeli military said on March 16 it had begun what it described as "limited ground operations" against Hezbollah in southern Lebanon.The announcement echoes similar statements issued in 2024, when Israel and Hezbollah fought a major war in Lebanon, and in 2023, when the mili...

stories.title: Israel pounds Beirut's southern suburbs with death toll raising to 850
stories.description: An Israeli strike hit Beirut's southern suburbs on March 15 according to Lebanese media. Israel's military issued an evacuation warning in the morning covering several neighbourhoods in the area. Lebanese authorities said the death toll in Israeli attacks rose to 850, while more ...

stories.title: Trump calls for help on securing Strait of Hormuz as oil prices surge
stories.description: US President Donald Trump is turning to other countries for help policing the Strait of Hormuz, effectively closed by Iran for the US and its allies after the US and Israel launched the war in Tehran. Since the closing of the crucial passageway, through which one fifth the world'...

stories.title: Trump announces AI chatbot ban while Pentagon uses it to prepare Iran attacks
stories.description: In the hours leading up to the US attack on Iran, Donald Trump announced that technology company Anthropic would be banned from all work with the federal government. At the same time, Anthropic's artificial intelligence chatbot Claude was reportedly being used by the Pentagon to ...

stories.title: Gulf States still under Iranian fire as Trump boasts of successful US strikes
stories.description: On the 17th day of the war, a ceasfire looks like a far off hope. While Donald Trump boasts of the success of the American strikes, which are supposed to have virtually destroyed Iranian military capabilities, the air defenses of the Gulf states—allies of the United States are op...

stories.title: India faces dilemma as energy crisis bites amid US trade deal negotiations
stories.description: New Delhi walks a policy tightrope, with public anger mounting over shortages of cooking gas as the US demands support for its war on Iran. Also in this edition: China's economy turns in stronger than expected figures, and oil prices continue to rise as Washington threatens to st...

stories.title: Middle East war live: Israel launches fresh wave of strikes across Iran
stories.description: Israel's military said on Monday it had launched a broad wave of strikes on the Iranian cities of Tehran, Shiraz and Tabriz. AFP reporters in Tehran said the city was pounded by heavy explosions through the night. Follow our liveblog for all the latest updates.

stories.title: Being an artist and a refugee: Amir Amiri and Parisa Karimi Molan speak to France 24
stories.description: France 24’s Gavin Lee speaks with Amir Amiri and Parisa Karimi, two Iranian musicians who now live in canada. They discuss the difficulties and hopes of being refugees and artists from a war-torn country.

stories.title: Turnout low in French mayoral elections seen as key test ahead of 2027 presidential race
stories.description: French voters cast ballots in municipal elections marred by low turnout on Sunday, in the last major test of the public mood ahead of next year’s all-important presidential election. The run-up to the vote was largely overshadowed by the Iran war and its fallout, notably the impa...

stories.title: From missiles strikes to rocketing oil price: Guy Laron speaks to France 24 to analyse the economic consequences of Iran war
stories.description: France 24’s Gavin Lee speaks with Guy Laron, Senior lecturer in international relations, the Hebrew University of Jerusalem, about the economic consequences of the ongoing war in Iran, from rising oil prices to how the strategic Strait of Hormuz can be used as an economic weapon.

stories.title: Trump administration threatens media outlets over Iran war coverage
stories.description: The head of the Federal Communications Commission, which oversees US media outlets, has warned that some broadcasters risk losing their licenses over their news coverage of the Iran war. Brendan Carr – a Trump appointee – wrote in a social media post that broadcasters must operat...

stories.title: How Israel, the US and Iran deploy covert ops
stories.description: Sometimes called sleeper cells, these anonymous agents employ deception to spy, sabotage or kill.

stories.title: Map shows how 16 days of attacks have evolved in US-Israel war on Iran
stories.description: Here's a day-by-day breakdown of attacks, illustrating the targets hit and the weapons used since February 28.

stories.title: The Iran war picture blackout
stories.description: Al Jazeera’s Linh Nguyen breaks down Planet Labs delaying the publication of its satellite imagery during an active war.

stories.title: How closing the Strait of Hormuz raises your grocery bill
stories.description: Closing the Strait of Hormuz raised oil prices. It also raises the cost of bread.

stories.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 17 of US-Israel attacks?
stories.description: Dubai contained a fire near its airport and temporarily suspended flights, while Trump claimed Iran wants to negotiate.

stories.title: Drone attack disrupts Dubai flights as Iran continues Gulf attacks
stories.description: At least one person killed in Abu Dhabi. Dubai flights disrupted, and fire breaks out in Fujairah after Iranian attacks.

stories.title: Japan begins release of oil reserves as Iran war sparks energy crisis
stories.description: Tokyo's move comes as oil prices remain elevated amid the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Large protest held in London against US-Israeli war on Iran
stories.description: Thousands of demonstrators took to the streets of London to oppose US-Israeli aggression against Iran, Lebanon and Gaza.

stories.title: Stars at Oscars 2026 call for end to war in Gaza and Iran
stories.description: Actors and filmmakers spoke out at the Oscars 2026 in Los Angeles against the US-Israeli war on Iran and the genocide.

stories.title: Nobody wants to get involved’ in Strait of Hormuz military operations
stories.description: Charles Kupchan says the US and allies are reluctant to use military means to safeguard the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Oil prices keep rising as Trump seeks coalition to reopen Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Brent crude tops $106 a barrel as markets see no end in sight to the halt of traffic through the critical waterway.

stories.title: US-Israeli strike pounds city of Hamadan, Iran
stories.description: Footage shows huge plumes of smoke from a US-Israeli strike on Hamadan, Iran.

stories.title: Trump says talks with Cuba ongoing, action possible after Iran
stories.description: US President Donald Trump said on Sunday that the United States could soon reach a deal with Cuba or take other action, signaling that developments in the long-strained relationship may come quickly. “Cuba also wants to make a deal, and I think we will pretty soon either make a d...

stories.title: Hundreds turn out for pro-Palestine rally in London
stories.description: Police arrest a protester for not removing a face cover during an annual protest held by a pro-Palestinian group in London on Sunday.—AFP LONDON: Hundreds of people turned out in London on Sunday for a pro-Palestinian march banned by the government after police said it was organi...

stories.title: Pope criticises those who invoke religion to wage war
stories.description: ROME: Pope Leo XIV renewed his appeal on Sunday for peace in the Middle East and criticised those who invoke religion to wage war, saying that “God cannot be enlisted by darkness”. “Currently, many of our brothers and sisters in the world are suffering from violent conflicts, cau...

stories.title: US, Chinese economic chiefs meet to pave way for Trump-Xi summit
stories.description: PARIS: Top US and Chinese economic officials launched a new round of talks in Paris on Sunday to iron out kinks in their trade truce and clear a smooth path for US President Donald Trump’s trip to Beijing to meet with Chinese President Xi Jinping at the end of March. The discussi...

stories.title: Israeli forces kill 16 more in Gaza, occupied West Bank
stories.description: Mustafa, who survived a gun attack by Israeli forces, is comforted at the funeral of his family.—AFP CAIRO: In a killing spree, Israeli forces shot dead 16 Palestinians, including two two children and their parents in the occupied West Bank and a man, his pregnant wife and their ...

stories.title: Iran warns other nations to stay away as US looks to expand war
stories.description: PEOPLE clear debris from homes damaged by US-Israel bombing in Tehran.—AFP • Trump asks allies to send ships to protect Hormuz shipping• Tehran turns down any chance of negotiations after US president says he’s not ready for a deal• Araghchi claims ‘ample evidence’ that bases acr...

stories.title: The environment — another casualty of war in Middle East
stories.description: • Experts say military hardware’s carbon footprint is staggering; conflicts’ carbon toll rivals yearly emissions of nations• Strikes spread toxins, polluting air, water, soil; toxic flames from burning oil depots and tankers pose direct threats, carry severe climate cost PARIS: F...

stories.title: Tehran reminds Modi about need to activate BRICS
stories.description: • Under India’s chairmanship, the expanded bloc has remained silent • Pezeshkian, Araghchi urge Indian counterparts to spearhead a ‘strong and constructive’ intervention • New Delhi caught in a diplomatic bind amid domestic LPG crisis; balances pro-Israel comments with its ‘frien...

stories.title: ‘I am dead … for coffee’: Israeli PM Netanyahu posts his video amid speculation about his death
stories.description: A video posted on Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s X account is being seen as his response to the rumours of his death, which were earlier debunked by his office. The video was posted on Sunday evening, in which Netanyahu is seen getting himself a coffee and being aske...

stories.title: US assures of ‘reliable’ energy supply to Asia-Pacific
stories.description: TOKYO: The United States can provide “reliable” energy supplies to Asia-Pacific, US Interior Secretary Doug Burgum said on Saturday, as the Middle East war cripples the region’s oil and gas flow. Oil and gas prices have surged since US-Israeli strikes on Iran killed its supreme l...

stories.title: Israeli bombing claims six lives in Gaza
stories.description: Smoke rises as the Israeli army demolishes a Palestinian house in Dura, a town in the Israeli-occupied West Bank.—Reuters GAZA CITY: Israeli strikes killed six people in Gaza Strip on Saturday, according to the territory’s civil defence agency. In a pre-dawn strike, two policemen...

stories.title: Five killed in Russian air attacks on Ukraine
stories.description: KYIV: Russia hammered Ukraine with missiles and drones on Saturday, killing five people and causing damage across several regions of the country, Ukrainian officials said. The main target was energy infrastructure outside the capital Kyiv, President Volodymyr Zelensky said, addin...

stories.title: Iran’s uranium enrichment secures independence, peaceful atom — supreme leader
stories.description: Ali Khamenei added that the US and other countries are not interested in Iran's progress and have put forward conditions presupposing a complete halt of uranium enrichment on Iranian territory

stories.title: Tehran could weigh nuclear deal with US with Iran-based enrichment consortium — Axios
stories.description: According to the report, a sixth round of talks on the Iranian nuclear program between the US president’s Special Envoy Steve Witkoff and Iran’s top diplomat, Abbas Araghchi, could be held this weekend in a Middle Eastern country

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
